note: Start the runtime-ownership run by proving current drop-in wire behavior still matches pinned Valkey. This is the compatibility floor for every later perf/architecture packet.
Capture the current pipeline-depth matrix before any runtime-ownership edits. Numbers are evidence, not a claim of parity.
Short stack-sampling run to confirm the post-0004595 hotspot shape before architecture packets fire. Uses smoke-size workloads so the loop can make progress unattended.

Architect-only. Do NOT implement the runtime rewrite. Read latest benchmark/profile evidence, map the faithful Valkey runtime ownership boundary, refine this packet graph if needed, and explicitly mark sharding / benchmark-only bypasses as non-goals. Any implementation packets you create must preserve normal dispatch, ACL, MULTI/EXEC, scripting, expiration, pub/sub, blocking wakeups, AOF, replication, and RDB semantics.

Add a wire-diff corpus script that stresses the semantics most likely to regress under runtime ownership: transaction order, WATCH invalidation, expiration-on-access, pub/sub non-interference if expressible in the corpus, blocking-command edge cases if safe/non-hanging, selected DB behavior, and script/replication-safe command ordering where supported. Do not edit existing corpus files or normalize away differences.

Fix only the CLIENT RESET / CLIENT GETNAME canary divergence. After RESET clears the client name, CLIENT GETNAME must return RESP nil, not an empty bulk string. Do not touch transaction or runtime-owner code. Focused gate: cargo build --bin redis-server && bash harness/oracle/smoke.sh --skip-build.

Fix only the nested MULTI canary divergence. A MULTI issued while already in MULTI must mark the transaction dirty so EXEC returns EXECABORT, matching upstream. Do not alter SELECT or owner-loop behavior in this packet. Focused gate: cargo build --bin redis-server && bash harness/oracle/smoke.sh --skip-build.

Fix only the queued SELECT inside MULTI/EXEC canary divergence. SELECT queued inside EXEC must affect later queued commands and the post-EXEC selected DB without applying at queue time. Preserve normal dispatch and do not implement RuntimeOwner here. If this requires a larger CommandContext redesign, leave a TODO(architect) and fail clearly instead of hacking around the oracle.
Run the full wire-diff corpus after the split runtime-owner canary fixers. If this fails, fix behavior or canary scope before any runtime scaffold work.

Create a typed RuntimeOwner scaffold without changing the default product path. Allowed: new runtime_owner module; types are exactly the rows owned by crates/redis-server/src/runtime_owner.rs in harness/architecture/object-vocabulary.tsv (authoritative — do not re-enumerate here so the two sources cannot drift); and tests proving construction/queue semantics for those types. Allowed in main.rs only: module declaration or clearly disabled wiring. Forbidden: adding any new workspace dep (no mio/polling/tokio/libc-epoll — the poller crate is a TODO(human) decision in harness/architecture/decisions/runtime-ownership.md), benchmark-only GET/SET/PING/INCR path, bypassing dispatch, disabling TLS/pubsub/blocking/replication/AOF/RDB, or making the scaffold default before oracle evidence exists. PollDriverHandle stays abstract (no concrete poller import) until human review. SlotId must be a newtype, not a bare u32 alias.
Compatibility gate after the inert runtime-owner scaffold. The scaffold is only acceptable if normal Valkey behavior remains byte-compatible on the surveyed corpus.
Refresh the profile matrix after the scaffold. Expected result may be neutral; this packet confirms no accidental slowdown and gives the next architect packet real evidence.
Longer sampled run after the safe scaffold. This is the evidence handoff for the next human/architect decision: proceed to real owner-loop migration, take a small allocator/hasher cleanup, or stop.

High-compute architect pass. Read the current code, benchmark/profile evidence, and docs/RUNTIME_OWNER_OVERNIGHT_ARCHITECTURE.md. Do not implement. Either strengthen the std-nonblocking owner-loop packet so it is faithful and bounded, or write a clear blocker. Keep the no benchmark-only bypass rule binding.

Implement the bounded std nonblocking owner-loop experiment locked by harness/architecture/decisions/runtime-ownership.md. Plain TCP only: replace the per-client read/dispatch/writer-thread path with one RuntimeOwner loop over nonblocking TcpListener/TcpStream slots; TLS stays on the existing thread-per-client path. Keep redis_commands::dispatch via CommandContext::with_server and parse_inline_or_multibulk_into; no command-specific fast paths, sharding, new poller dependency, raw poller unsafe, or disabled features. Transitional DB rule: use existing global_databases() Arc<Mutex<RedisDb>> handles as the live DB source, holding selected DB guards only for bounded dispatch batches; do not create a divergent live Vec<RedisDb>. Foreign bytes for pub/sub, blocked wakeups, WAIT/replication, and similar routes must enter through per-slot mpsc receivers drained by the owner loop, not foreign socket writes. Preserve maxclients, client-info, connected-client metrics, selected DB, RESET/QUIT, pubsub/replica/blocked cleanup. If local wire-smoke cannot be restored in the packet, stop with TODO(architect) instead of weakening behavior.
Full wire-smoke after the first plain-TCP owner loop. This must be green before any benchmark number is trusted.
Benchmark the default product path after the owner loop is green. Update evidence only; do not hand-edit benchmark docs unless the runner owns that artifact.
Profile the owner loop after the first real benchmark. The next perf packet must use this evidence, not guesses.

One bounded polish packet after owner-loop profile evidence exists. Improve the measured owner-loop bottleneck without bypassing normal dispatch or command semantics. Candidate areas: write flushing, query-buffer draining, per-tick batching, client slot iteration, or reply buffer movement. Do not add sharding or command-specific fast paths.
Full wire-smoke after owner-loop polish.
Final overnight profile matrix after owner-loop polish.
Final overnight hotspot profile. This is the handoff for the next architecture session.
Start the next performance push by reproving current wire compatibility at HEAD. No benchmark or architecture packet counts unless this stays green.
Fresh profile matrix at current HEAD after the runtime-owner polish. This is the pre-poller baseline for the next push.
Long p100 sampled hotspot run at current HEAD. The following architect packet must use this evidence and not throughput vibes.

Add a typed json_command runner for raw call-tree/flamegraph-style profiling artifacts. On macOS it may use /usr/bin/sample; on Linux it may use perf/cargo-flamegraph if available. It must emit RunnerResult JSON, attach raw artifacts under harness/bench/results or harness/bench/profiles, include hardware/tool metadata, and not change server behavior or benchmark methodology outside this runner.
Run the new call-tree/flamegraph artifact profiler before poller implementation. This should produce raw profiler artifacts plus a small typed summary for the architect packet.

High-compute architect pass for the next wall. Read the current code and all runtime-owner-6 evidence. Either approve a concrete mio-readiness poller packet for plain TCP or block with a better packet graph. Do not implement. Keep normal redis_commands::dispatch, RESP parser, ACL, transactions, scripting, expiration, pub/sub, blocking, AOF, RDB, and replication in scope. No sharding, owner-owned DB migration, TLS migration, or benchmark-only fast paths in this packet family.

Implement the architect-approved mio readiness poller for the default plain-TCP RuntimeOwner path. Add mio as a workspace dependency with os-poll and net features. Replace the std linear accept/read/write slot scan with mio::Poll, mio::Events, and stable slot tokens: listener readiness accepts until WouldBlock; client readability drains socket reads and dispatches parsed commands; writable interest is enabled only while ClientWriteBuffer has pending bytes and removed when empty. Slots that hit MAX_COMMANDS_PER_SLOT_TICK with complete commands still buffered must be rescheduled by the owner loop without waiting for a new socket readiness edge. Keep one synchronous owner thread and keep redis_commands::dispatch via CommandContext::with_server and parse_inline_or_multibulk_into as the only command path. Keep global_databases() as transitional storage; no owner-owned live Vec<RedisDb>. Foreign pub/sub, blocked wakeup, WAIT/replication, and similar bytes stay on per-slot mpsc receivers; drain them after poll returns and on a short bounded timeout, queue owner write interest, and do not let foreign threads write owner sockets. Preserve maxclients, client-info, connected-client metrics, selected DB, RESET/QUIT, pub/sub cleanup, replica cleanup, blocked-key cleanup, ACL, MULTI/EXEC, scripting, expiration, AOF, RDB, and replication semantics. No tokio, polling crate, raw epoll/kqueue/libc poller code, unsafe poller budget increase, sharding, TLS migration, command-specific fast paths, disabled background semantics, or public performance claim. Gate with cargo check/test plus full wire-smoke; if compatibility cannot be restored, stop with TODO(architect) rather than weakening behavior.
Full wire-smoke after the poller owner-loop packet. Benchmarks only count if this passes.
Post-poller profile matrix on the default product path.
Post-poller p100 hotspot profile. Use this to decide whether the next wall is owner-owned DB, parser/serializer, allocation/copy churn, or command dispatch.
Post-poller call-tree/flamegraph artifact run. This should make the next packet choice auditable.
Guard against a large p100 PING throughput-ratio regression after the poller packet. Threshold is configured in harness/runners.toml.
Guard against a large p100 GET throughput-ratio regression after the poller packet. Threshold is configured in harness/runners.toml.
Guard against a large p100 SET throughput-ratio regression after the poller packet. Threshold is configured in harness/runners.toml.
Guard against a large p100 INCR throughput-ratio regression after the poller packet. Threshold is configured in harness/runners.toml.

Manual follow-up only. Use post-poller evidence to scope the full owner-owned DB migration. Do not implement. This packet should decompose selected DB ownership, active expire, AOF, replication, RDB, scripting, blocking, pub/sub, WATCH/MULTI, and TLS interaction before any code packet is allowed.

Reduce avoidable command timing / slowlog predicate overhead shown in post-poller calltree evidence. Upstream still times every command in server.c::call(), so do not disable slowlog, command duration semantics, latency/accounting, AOF argv snapshots, replication propagation, ACL, maxmemory, readonly gates, or normal redis_commands::dispatch. Prefer a cheap live-config predicate/cache or consolidated duration capture. No command-specific PING/GET/SET/INCR fast paths. Local gates: cargo check/tests for touched crates plus full wire-smoke before benchmarks.
Wire-smoke after command timing / slowlog hot-path work. Benchmarks only count if this passes.
Profile matrix after command timing / slowlog hot-path work.
Call-tree evidence after command timing / slowlog hot-path work. The next packet must use this evidence, not assumptions.

Reduce hot reply encoding allocation/copy overhead shown in GET/SET/PING calltrees. Add direct Client/CommandContext helpers for common RESP2 legacy replies where safe: simple string, integer, null bulk, bulk bytes, array header. Preserve generic write_frame/RESP3 native frame behavior, exact wire bytes, error replies, pub/sub push frames, and command APIs. No command-specific benchmark bypass; commands should still call CommandContext reply helpers. Local gates: existing exact reply unit tests plus full wire-smoke.
Wire-smoke after direct reply-buffer hot-path work.
Profile matrix after direct reply-buffer hot-path work.
Call-tree evidence after direct reply-buffer hot-path work. The next packet must inspect whether encode_resp2/RawVec/malloc samples moved.

Add a faithful no-watch fast path for dirty-key signaling. Current SET/INCR calltrees show RedisDb::signal_modified -> watched_keys_touch -> global mutex/allocation even when no clients watch keys. Match upstream touchWatchedKey's early return shape: skip lock/allocation when no watchers exist, but preserve WATCH/MULTI/EXEC dirty-CAS behavior when watchers exist, including selected DB and expired watched-key semantics already covered by canaries. Do not disable WATCH or route around normal RedisDb::set_key/sync_delete signaling. Add or strengthen a wire canary if coverage is missing.
Wire-smoke after WATCH dirty-key fast path. Runtime-owner WATCH canaries must remain green.
Profile matrix after WATCH dirty-key fast path.
Final call-tree evidence after the three small hot-path packets. Use this to decide whether to return to owner-owned DB architecture.

Guard against a large p100 PING throughput-ratio regression after the small hot-path wave. Run these regression packets sequentially or through the scheduler resource lock; runner auto-commits share git state.
Guard against a large p100 GET throughput-ratio regression after the small hot-path wave.
Guard against a large p100 SET throughput-ratio regression after the small hot-path wave.
Guard against a large p100 INCR throughput-ratio regression after the small hot-path wave.

Add owner-compatible selected-DB and cross-DB routing APIs to CommandContext while keeping the current live storage model. Preserve redis_commands::dispatch as the only command path. Existing borrowed-DB constructors may remain for tests and legacy startup/TLS surfaces, but owner-loop dispatch should be able to pass a DB-list route keyed by Client::db_index. SELECT validation must use the configured DB count rather than a hard-coded local assumption where the context can provide it. Do not move the live DB list into RuntimeOwner yet and do not introduce a second live keyspace.
Full wire-smoke after CommandContext DB-routing groundwork. The next cross-DB packet is not trusted unless this passes.

Remove direct global_databases() command access from MOVE, COPY, SWAPDB, queued EXEC selected-DB routing, and blocked wake paths by using the CommandContext/owner DB-list APIs. Preserve Valkey semantics: SELECT is per-client, WATCH invalidation is per selected DB, MOVE/COPY signal the correct DBs, and SWAPDB swaps keyspace contents without swapping watched/blocking/ready-key ownership structures. Add or strengthen runtime-owner canaries for SELECT plus MOVE/COPY/SWAPDB/WATCH interactions. Do not flip RuntimeOwner to own the live Vec<RedisDb> in this packet.
Full wire-smoke after cross-DB owner-routing work, including runtime-owner canaries. The live DB owner must not flip until this passes.

Move the default command-execution keyspace to a RuntimeOwner-owned Vec<RedisDb>. Main/startup may load RDB/AOF into the owner DB vector before the owner loop starts. RuntimeOwner dispatch must use the CommandContext DB-list route and must not use global_databases() as a normal command store. Active expire must run in the owner cron/expire step or through an explicit owner event. TLS command execution, replication apply, runtime AOF replay, and persistence snapshot entry must route through owner-owned DB APIs/channels or stop with TODO(architect)/TODO(human); do not leave any of them mutating a divergent global DB. Preserve redis_commands::dispatch, selected DB, WATCH/MULTI/EXEC, blocking, pub/sub, scripting, ACL, maxmemory, readonly-replica, AOF, replication, RDB, and RESP semantics. No TLS socket migration is required in this packet, but no TLS/global-DB divergence is allowed after the flip.
Full wire-smoke after the owner-owned DB loop flip. Benchmarks only count if this passes.
Profile matrix after RuntimeOwner owns the live DB vector. This is telemetry only, not a public speed claim.
Call-tree evidence after the owner-owned DB loop flip. Confirm normal command execution no longer samples the transitional global DB lock path and identify the next architecture wall.
Guard against a large p100 PING throughput-ratio regression after the owner-owned DB loop flip.
Guard against a large p100 GET throughput-ratio regression after the owner-owned DB loop flip.
Guard against a large p100 SET throughput-ratio regression after the owner-owned DB loop flip.
Guard against a large p100 INCR throughput-ratio regression after the owner-owned DB loop flip.
Telemetry-only survey of currently unswept upstream TCL unit files with per-file timeouts. Generates evidence and packet candidates; does not create a public conformance claim by itself.

Focused official-suite packet. Current survey: unit/geo reaches Test Summary at 62 pass / 9 fail. Fix the counted edge semantics only: missing member / FROMMEMBER-FROMLONLAT / BYRADIUS-BYBOX-BYPOLYGON / ANY-without-COUNT / COUNT>0 / store-option incompatibilities / BYPOLYGON vertex-count behavior. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 90 --files unit/geo. Do not broaden into geo performance or unrelated zset work.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after the geo edge-semantics packet. Evidence only; do not hand-edit conformance claims.

Focused official-suite packet. Current survey aborts unit/bitops at 'BITCOUNT with just start' because BITCOUNT key start returns ERR syntax error. Match upstream BITCOUNT key [start [end [BIT|BYTE]]] and BITPOS key bit [start [end [BIT|BYTE]]] parsing/error behavior from bitops.c. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 90 --files unit/bitops. Do not touch BITFIELD here.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after the BITCOUNT/BITPOS packet.

Focused official-suite packet. Current survey aborts unit/scan at ZSCAN ... COUNT 1000 NOSCORES with ERR syntax error. Match Valkey 8/9 scanOptions NOSCORES handling: NOSCORES is accepted for ZSCAN only and suppresses score replies; other scan families reject it. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 90 --files unit/scan.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after the ZSCAN NOSCORES packet.

Stability-focused official-suite packet. Current survey aborts unit/bitfield at 'BITFIELD overflow detection fuzzing' with I/O error reading reply, suggesting a server panic or connection loss. Fix only overflow parsing/arithmetic stability and upstream-compatible overflow FAIL/SAT/WRAP behavior. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 90 --files unit/bitfield. Do not broaden into BITCOUNT/BITPOS.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after the BITFIELD overflow-stability packet.

Start the 2026-05-23 overnight TCL frontier with a fresh survey after the manual DUMP/RESTORE closeout. Expected baseline: 173 counted passes, 0 counted failures, 4 no-summary files. This is telemetry only and should not edit product code.

Focused HLL packet. `pfdebug_command` already exists in `crates/redis-commands/src/hyperloglog.rs`; unit/hyperloglog currently aborts because PFDEBUG is unknown. Wire PFDEBUG into dispatch and fix only the immediate TCL-facing PFDEBUG semantics needed to advance the file. First target is `PFDEBUG GETREG hll` returning an array of 16384 registers after PFADD. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 90 --files unit/hyperloglog. Do not rewrite PFADD/PFCOUNT/PFMERGE or invent a second HLL representation.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after the PFDEBUG dispatch packet. This gate decides whether the next HLL frontier is real sparse/dense semantics or the file is counted-green.

Wire the already-translated SORT/SORT_RO module into the crate and repair compile/API gaps until unit/sort advances past unknown-command and obvious translation issues. Preserve upstream SORT semantics from sort.c: BY, GET, LIMIT, ASC/DESC, ALPHA, STORE, and SORT_RO rejecting STORE. Scope cap: if STORE or hash-pattern lookup needs a larger CommandContext design, land a faithful read-only subset plus an explicit blocker rather than returning fake data. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 120 --files unit/sort. Do not special-case the tests; use RedisObject collection iterators and normal db access.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after SORT/SORT_RO wiring.

Fix SLOWLOG core edge semantics that do not require FUNCTION/FCALL. Known frontiers from unit/slowlog: `SLOWLOG GET -2` must error with `ERR count should be greater than or equal to -1`; rewritten commands should log original argv where the command path already retains it; blocked BLPOP should be logged only after unblocked if feasible from the existing blocked-command wake path. Do not implement full scripting functions here. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 120 --files unit/slowlog.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after SLOWLOG core edge fixes.

Implement a minimal faithful FUNCTION LOAD [REPLACE] plus FCALL/FCALL_RO bridge backed by the existing EVAL machinery. Target the TCL harness pattern: `#!lua name=<library>` with `redis.register_function('name', function(KEYS, ARGV) <body> end)` or `server.register_function(...)`, followed by `FCALL name numkeys ...`. FUNCTION LOAD must return the library name as a bulk string; unknown/malformed code should error instead of pretending to load. Scope cap: this is not full Valkey functions, engine metadata, persistence, async kill, or libraries API. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 120 --files unit/scripting,unit/slowlog.
Final overnight frontier survey after minimal FUNCTION LOAD / FCALL support. If this passes substantially more scripting tests, update docs through a follow-up doc packet, not by hand-waving.
Restart the TCL frontier after the HLL/SORT closeout commit. This v2 baseline deliberately breaks the old serial dependency chain around the blocked tcl-sort-wire-minimal packet.

Focused SORT follow-up. SORT/SORT_RO are already wired and compile at HEAD; do not redo that packet. Fix the next observed unit/sort frontier only: COMMAND GETKEYS for SORT/SORT_RO and the legacy CONFIG GET/SET alias list-max-ziplist-size mapping to list-max-listpack-size. Preserve normal SORT execution semantics and do not special-case test names. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 120 --files unit/sort.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after SORT connection/config metadata fixes.

Focused HLL follow-up. PFDEBUG is already wired; unit/hyperloglog now reports 23 pass / 3 fail. Wire PFSELFTEST and make hll-sparse-max-bytes CONFIG SET affect PFADD sparse-to-dense promotion through the live config path. Do not rewrite PFCOUNT/PFMERGE or invent a second HLL representation. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 120 --files unit/hyperloglog.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after PFSELFTEST and hll-sparse-max-bytes live-config fixes.

Focused SLOWLOG edge packet independent of functions. Fix count validation (`SLOWLOG GET -2`), original-argv logging for already-supported rewritten commands where the port preserves the original argv, and blocked BLPOP slowlog timing if the existing blocked-command wake path exposes a clean hook. Do not implement FUNCTION/FCALL here. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 120 --files unit/slowlog.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after SLOWLOG core edge fixes.

Minimal faithful FUNCTION LOAD [REPLACE] plus FCALL/FCALL_RO bridge backed by the existing EVAL/Lua machinery. Target the TCL harness patterns: `#!lua name=<library>` with redis/server.register_function in function-call or table-call form, then FCALL name numkeys ... . FUNCTION LOAD must return the library name as a bulk string and malformed code must error; no fake OK. Scope cap: not full Valkey functions metadata, persistence, async kill, library listing, engines API, or cluster behavior. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 150 --files unit/scripting,unit/slowlog.
Refresh the unswept TCL survey after minimal FUNCTION LOAD / FCALL support.
Broader single-node core TCL survey after the branch-style frontier push. This is the evidence handoff for the next packet-generation cycle, not a public compatibility claim by itself.

Fresh clean-commit baseline after 80f7dd5. Expected focused frontier: bitops/bitfield/geo/hll/scan/dump/slowlog green, sort 49/54, scripting aborts at WAITAOF. This packet exists to prevent stale blocked rows from driving the next run.

allowed_collateral.why: SORT STORE uses the same list representation persisted by AOF rewrite.

note: Fix only the five remaining unit/sort failures observed at 20260523T154306Z: SORT STORE list encoding should match upstream quicklist reporting, SORT sorted-set BY nosort from scripts should return the same arrays as direct SORT, and bad-double numeric sort errors should match the upstream ERR/double pattern. QuickList storage changes may require AOF rewrite collateral because persisted list iteration must support the same list representation. Do not special-case test names or bypass normal SORT/Lua reply conversion. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 120 --files unit/sort.
Refresh the focused TCL survey after the SORT final-five packet.

Fix the first two observed unit/scripting semantic failures without broad function-engine work: Redis multi-bulk replies must convert to Lua table values with upstream-compatible shape, and SELECT inside Lua must not leak the selected DB back to the caller after the script/function returns. Preserve normal dispatch and do not fake script return values. Reproduce with: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 150 --files unit/scripting.
Refresh the focused TCL survey after Lua reply conversion and selected-DB restoration fixes.

Fix the next scripting frontier around WAIT and WAITAOF in scripts/functions. In the current single-node non-replication envelope, script-safe WAIT/WAITAOF should not block and should return zero through normal command dispatch/pcall paths. replication.rs is the canonical owner of WAIT/WAITAOF, so it is an explicit target; eval.rs only owns inner-script deny_blocking and script error-boundary conversion. Do not implement full replication durability or weaken unrelated command semantics. If the latest survey stops before WAIT, regenerate packet scope from that evidence instead of forcing this packet. Reproduce with: cargo build --bin redis-server && cd reference/valkey && VALKEY_BIN_DIR=$PWD/../../target/debug tclsh tests/test_helper.tcl --single unit/scripting --clients 1 --skip-leaks --tags "-needs:repl -needs:debug -external:skip" --quiet --baseport 31111 --portcount 8000.
Refresh the focused TCL survey after WAIT/WAITAOF scripting semantics.

Next scripting frontier after WAIT/WAITAOF: install a minimal Redis-compatible cjson table in the Lua sandbox so unit/scripting reaches past JSON numeric/string/round-trip cases. Use serde_json already present in the workspace; do not add a new dependency and do not broaden into cmsgpack/bitop unless the cjson tests require it. Reproduce with the custom-baseport TCL command from docs/TCL_NEXT_FRONTIER_20260523.md while another survey holds the default port range.
Refresh unit/scripting after the cjson sandbox packet. If the next abort is cmsgpack or bitop, generate a sibling embedded-library packet rather than expanding cjson retroactively.
Broader single-node core TCL survey after the v3 SORT and scripting packets. This is the evidence handoff for the next packet-generation cycle.

Baseline the source-shaped persistence frontier before any more persistence edits. This runner should complete even when scenarios fail; individual scenario rows are the signal. The next implementation packet must target the first red scenario cluster rather than generic command success.
Guard the already-working RDB restart cycle before persistence fixers run.
Guard the AOF restart cycle before persistence fixers run.
Guard AOF rewrite-under-write-load before persistence fixers run.

Manual fallback only after 20260523T193229Z baseline showed the DEBUG reload/load scenarios green. If a later frontier regresses them, fix only DEBUG RELOAD / DEBUG LOADAOF restart semantics. Required focused gate: python3 harness/oracle/persistence-frontier.py --skip-build --scenarios rdb-debug-reload-complex-dataset,aof-debug-loadaof-complex-dataset,expires-after-rdb-reload,expires-after-aof-loadaof --fail-on-failure. Also keep python3 harness/oracle/rdb-diff --direction=all green. Do not fake +OK: the tests must compare data after reload.
Manual fallback refresh after DEBUG reload/load fixes.

Manual fallback only after 20260523T193229Z baseline showed strict/truncated/unknown AOF startup scenarios green. If a later frontier regresses them, fix strict AOF replay startup behavior around truncated files and unknown commands. Required focused gate: python3 harness/oracle/persistence-frontier.py --skip-build --scenarios aof-load-truncated-yes-short-read,aof-load-truncated-no-fails,aof-unknown-command-fails-startup --fail-on-failure. Startup must fail closed for corrupt AOF when configured; do not silently skip bad commands.
Manual fallback refresh after strict/truncated AOF load fixes.

Fix AOF propagation/replay edge cases only. Current baseline red case: GETEX without options appends to AOF (size 54 -> 78) but upstream integration/aof.tcl says it must not. SPOP count and LMPOP/ZMPOP replay are currently green and must stay green. Required focused gate: python3 harness/oracle/persistence-frontier.py --skip-build --scenarios getex-does-not-append-to-aof,aof-spop-count-replay,aof-lmpop-zmpop-replay --fail-on-failure. Preserve normal command dispatch; do not add benchmark-only paths or fake replay results.
Refresh the persistence frontier after AOF propagation fixes.

Manual fallback only after 20260523T193229Z baseline showed collection rewrite digest green. If a later frontier regresses it, fix AOF rewrite digest parity for normal collection types. Required focused gate: python3 harness/oracle/persistence-frontier.py --skip-build --scenarios aof-rewrite-collections-digest --fail-on-failure plus existing persistence-aof-rewrite-cycle. Do not implement multi-part manifest here; if the safe blocking rewrite is insufficient, document the remaining architecture wall.
Manual fallback refresh after AOF rewrite digest fixes.

Architect-only. Do not implement multi-part AOF in this packet. Use the frontier result for multipart-aof-manifest-basic-load and upstream aof-multi-part.tcl to decide whether to add a bounded manifest implementation wave. Output should be a packet graph for manifest parse/load, base+incr replay, BGREWRITEAOF finalization, and explicit non-goals. If old-style AOF still has red scenarios, block manifest work and send the loop back to those first.

Expand the JSON persistence frontier with source-shaped multi-part AOF scenarios before implementation work relies on it. Preserve the existing multipart-aof-manifest-basic-load scenario and add typed rows for missing manifest file target, non-monotonic INCR seq, blank/empty/duplicate/unknown-type manifest failures, empty dir startup, discontinuous INCR load, empty INCR load, appendonly-enable layout creation, and rewrite-finalization sequence advance. The runner must still complete and emit per-scenario measurements when scenarios fail; do not normalize failures away or require the external TCL harness for this proof.

Baseline the expanded multi-part AOF frontier. This should complete even with red manifest scenarios; implementation packets must target the red scenario clusters rather than changing runner accounting.

Implement manifest parse/load and base+incr replay only. Add an AOF manifest model owned by redis-commands/src/aof.rs, resolve files under appenddirname, validate Valkey's manifest grammar, load at most one BASE followed by ordered INCR files, support BASE .aof through the strict AOF loader and BASE .rdb through the RDB loader, and fail startup for missing required files or invalid manifests. Keep legacy single appendfilename fallback when no manifest exists. Do not change BGREWRITEAOF finalization, CONFIG SET appendonly, or history deletion in this packet. Required focused gate: python3 harness/oracle/persistence-frontier.py --skip-build --scenarios multipart-aof-manifest-basic-load,multipart-aof-manifest-discontinuous-incr-load,multipart-aof-manifest-empty-incr-load,multipart-aof-manifest-missing-file-fails,multipart-aof-manifest-invalid-format-fails --fail-on-failure.
Refresh the persistence frontier after manifest parse/load and base+incr replay land.

Move the active AOF writer to the manifest current-INCR layout. On startup or CONFIG SET appendonly yes, create appenddirname if needed, create/persist appendfilename.manifest, create an initial BASE if Valkey would, open the last/new INCR with O_APPEND semantics, and keep appending only to that INCR. Preserve existing strict AOF replay and CONFIG semantics. Do not implement BGREWRITEAOF manifest finalization or async history deletion here. Required gates: python3 harness/oracle/persistence-cycle.py --mode aof and python3 harness/oracle/persistence-frontier.py --skip-build --scenarios multipart-aof-manifest-empty-dir-startup,multipart-aof-appendonly-enable-layout --fail-on-failure.
Refresh the persistence frontier after manifest current-INCR writer creation and appendonly-enable layout handling.

Implement Valkey-shaped BGREWRITEAOF manifest finalization. Before writing the new BASE, switch future appends to a new INCR; write a durable temp BASE using the configured AOF/RDB preamble mode; after success, rename BASE, persist a manifest naming the new BASE plus current INCR, and keep the active writer on the INCR. It may remain synchronous for v1, but no child/thread may rename over the active writer and no acknowledged write may be lost. History file deletion and repeated failed-rewrite INCR accumulation are non-goals unless covered by the expanded frontier first. Required gates: python3 harness/oracle/persistence-cycle.py --mode aof-rewrite and python3 harness/oracle/persistence-frontier.py --skip-build --scenarios aof-rewrite-collections-digest,multipart-aof-rewrite-sequence-advance --fail-on-failure.
Guard rewrite-under-write-load after manifest finalization replaces the single-file blocking rewrite path.
Final JSON persistence frontier refresh for the manifest wave before the broader focused TCL persistence survey.

Run the focused upstream persistence TCL files after the manifest wave. This runner is broader telemetry, not the manifest proof; manifest proof is the persistence-frontier and rewrite-cycle evidence above.
Ledger the corrected source-shaped persistence frontier after the GETEX AOF propagation oracle was updated to inspect the manifest current-INCR path instead of the legacy root appendonly.aof path.

Baseline the current red/no-summary/timeout single-node TCL files before breadth implementation. This is telemetry; pass status means the runner completed and preserved per-file logs, not that all files are green.

Scout the largest single-node files that were previously skipped-by-policy. Do not treat red output as failure of the run; use the logs to confirm packet ordering, especially hashexpire/introspection/ACL/tracking/wait.

Remove the explicit ZRANGE BYLEX stub by routing ZRANGE key min max BYLEX [REV] [LIMIT ...] through the existing lex-range helpers and preserving upstream syntax rules. Also verify ZRANGESTORE RESP3/BYLEX behavior still works. Required local proof: cargo test -p redis-commands zset and a focused TCL run for unit/type/zset.

Implement HGETDEL key FIELDS num field ... only. Reply with an array of old values/nils, remove found fields, delete empty hash key, preserve WRONGTYPE/syntax semantics, and add the dispatch entry. Do not implement field TTL in this packet.

Install a Redis-compatible cjson table into the mlua sandbox. Minimum: encode/decode JSON objects/arrays/scalars/null, cjson.null sentinel, and the cjson configuration functions used in unit/scripting. Use serde_json already present in redis-commands. Preserve one-line RESP-safe script error conversion.

Implement cheap CLIENT/HELLO/introspection unblocks: CONFIG SET/GET availability-zone with HELLO availability_zone field, CLIENT INFO current-client output, CLIENT TRACKING/CACHING/GETREDIR honest single-node state, and CLIENT import-source no-op state if needed by expire/import-mode tests. Do not implement full client-side invalidation routing.

Start from the latest unit/type/list timeout log. Fix the first concrete blocking/wake or runner-tag issue only; do not rewrite the list implementation. If no precise failure can be isolated, record packet_blocked with the log path and move on.
Refresh the current-red TCL frontier after the first breadth implementation wave. Red files are acceptable; the goal is to convert aborts/timeouts to counted summaries and identify the next abort.

Improve the existing minimal FUNCTION registry enough for early unit/functions: case-insensitive function lookup, library name validation, unknown engine errors, FUNCTION LIST metadata, FUNCTION STATS counts, and FCALL/FCALL_RO preserving existing dispatch. Do not implement replication, long-running kill, or full dump/restore unless reached cheaply after early summary output.

Fix timeout-causing pub/sub protocol edges: CLIENT REPLY OFF|ON|SKIP suppresses replies at the flush boundary, Pub/Sub PING RESP2 envelope matches upstream, and keyspace/keyevent notifications are delivered through the existing registry when notify-keyspace-events is configured. Do not rewrite pubsub registry ownership.

Target the measured stream aborts/timeouts only: XADD MAXLEN ~ and LIMIT trim semantics, XREAD wake suppression for XADD+DEL cases, and PEL reassignment after XGROUP SETID. Current inline stream storage may remain if observable behavior matches the frontier.

allowed_collateral.why: Only if basic field-expiry behavior requires ignoring or preserving HASH_2 payloads without corrupting load.
Only if tests require explicit HASH_2 non-support handling; byte-perfect persistence is not part of this packet.

note: Add a pragmatic per-field hash expiry data model and basic HGETEX/HSETEX/HEXPIRE/HPEXPIRE/HEXPIREAT/HPEXPIREAT/HTTL/HPTTL/HEXPIRETIME/HPEXPIRETIME/HPERSIST behavior. Non-goals: full active-expire parity, replication propagation, and byte-perfect RDB HASH_2 persistence. If too large, land data model plus HGETEX/HSETEX first and record the split.
Run the expanded single-node core TCL sweep after the second breadth wave. This is the main morning scoreboard; failures are useful if they are counted and attached to logs.
Refresh full-suite accounting after the breadth run so docs/dashboard can show movement across pass/no-summary/timeout/skipped buckets against the 4,299-test denominator.

Unlock the cmsgpack abort in unit/scripting by installing a Redis-compatible cmsgpack/cmsgpack_safe table into the mlua sandbox. Minimum scope: pack/unpack for nil/bool/int/float/string/binary/table arrays/maps as used by upstream TCL; preserve Redis byte payloads and one-line RESP-safe script error conversion. Do not redesign the Lua runtime or add a native module ABI.

Unlock unit/type/string's SET IFEQ abort. Implement upstream-compatible SET key value IFEQ comparison-value semantics, including interaction with GET/NX/XX/EX/PX/KEEPTTL parser rules and nil/no-op reply behavior. Avoid broad string rewrites; run focused string command tests plus the TCL string file if possible.

Deepen the existing minimal FUNCTION implementation enough to stop early unit/functions aborts: source-shaped library metadata, case-insensitive function lookup, name validation, unknown engine errors, FUNCTION LIST shape, FUNCTION STATS counters, and FCALL/FCALL_RO compatibility. Explicit non-goals: replication, long-running kill, full dump/restore, and cluster/function propagation.

Unlock CLIENT LIST/CLIENT INFO/COMMAND introspection aborts. Implement the common CLIENT LIST filter syntax and output fields used by upstream TCL (ID, ADDR, LADDR, TYPE, USER, SKIPME, MAXAGE, IP, CAPA as feasible), plus early COMMAND GETKEYS/GETKEYSANDFLAGS semantics. Keep the model single-node; do not fake cluster or module command tables.

Unlock small admin/observability files rather than making them perfect: COMMANDLOG HELP/LIST/RESET basics, LATENCY HISTOGRAM shape used by tests, and MONITOR command availability/basic streaming behavior that does not hang the suite. Prefer honest minimal single-node behavior over broad fake observability.

Unlock unit/tracking by implementing a minimal but coherent client-side tracking state machine: CLIENT TRACKING ON/OFF, TRACKINGINFO, REDIRECT/BCAST/PREFIX/OPTIN/OPTOUT/NOLOOP parsing and visible state. Full invalidation routing is optional only if reached safely; if deferred, document exact non-goals and avoid hanging tests.

allowed_collateral.why: Only to use already-landed Redis-DS listpack helpers from quicklist/list command code.
Only if the current list timeout is actually a blocking wake/runtime-owner scheduling issue.

note: Revisit unit/type/list after the Redis-DS quicklist/listpack source-shaped wave. Goal is to convert the timeout into counted pass/fail by fixing the first real hang or blocking wake issue, and opportunistically route list storage through quicklist helpers only if it is clean. Do not attempt a full object-model migration unless the focused gates stay green.
Run the expanded single-node core TCL sweep after the unlock wave. This is the all-day scoreboard: success means the runner completes with attached logs, not that every upstream file is green.
Refresh full-suite accounting after the unlock wave so the morning review can compare pass/fail/no-summary/timeout/skipped against the 4,299-test denominator.

Materialize the post-unlock single-node-core dashboard from latest inventory and TCL survey results. This is the morning summary artifact for proved / known-fail / abort / timeout / not-swept movement.

Side-worktree packet. Unlock the current unit/scripting abort at missing global `bit` by installing a Redis-compatible bit table into the mlua sandbox. Implement 32-bit wrapping semantics for tobit, band, bor, bxor, bnot, lshift, rshift, arshift, and rotate helpers if reached. Do not redesign scripting/functions or touch list/quicklist/object code. Required gate: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 150 --files unit/scripting.

allowed_collateral.why: Only if tracking runtime counters need to expose per-client state already owned by ClientTrackingState.

note: Follow-up to the completed minimal tracking packet. Export real packet-level tracking counters in INFO: tracking_total_items, tracking_total_keys, tracking_total_prefixes, and tracking_clients. Use the live tracking runtime from redis-core/src/tracking.rs; do not fake constants. Then run unit/tracking to see the next concrete frontier. Non-goals: complete script read-key introspection and exact invalidation interleaving for every command family.

Unlock unit/type/string's current LCS abort by porting Valkey's LCS command shape. Implement LCS key1 key2, LEN, IDX, WITHMATCHLEN, and MINMATCHLEN as used by the upstream string tests. Preserve Redis byte strings and upstream reply shape. Add dispatch if missing. Scope cap: dynamic-programming implementation is acceptable; no SIMD or optimization work.

allowed_collateral.why: Only if the replication stream counter/offset state is owned there already.
Only if the current attach_to_replication_stream support requires plain TCP replica handshake plumbing.

note: Target the current unit/hashexpire abort: attach_to_replication_stream sees +FULLRESYNC as the command count, so the focused replication-stream helper cannot observe validation propagation. Implement the minimal single-node replication stream behavior needed for hashexpire validation-argument tests, preserving normal command propagation for HSETEX/HGETEX/HEXPIRE validation paths. Do not claim full replica data sync or cluster replication. Required gate: python3 harness/oracle/tcl-survey.py --skip-build --timeout-s 150 --files unit/hashexpire.
Refresh the expanded single-node core TCL sweep after wave C. This is the next high-denominator scoreboard; success means every selected file has attached logs and no parser-hidden summaries.
Refresh full-suite accounting after wave C against the full 4,299-test denominator.
Materialize the post-wave-C single-node-core dashboard from latest inventory and TCL survey results.

allowed_collateral.why: Only if canonical exports need to expose ListPack helper types.
Update draft status if the parked listpack draft is consumed.

note: Canonicalize ListPack first. Merge the useful existing source-draft into crates/redis-ds/src/listpack.rs, add pub(crate) data storage or equivalent safe byte-buffer owner, remove duplicate owner drift, and add focused append/get/delete/seek/validate tests. Do not wire RedisObject encodings yet. Required proof: cargo test -p redis-ds listpack and cargo check --workspace.

allowed_collateral.why: Update generated draft status if consumed.

note: Integrate the generated IntSet draft into the canonical owner. Preserve Redis byte semantics, no unsafe, no external deps. Public surface should cover new/add/remove/find/random/min/max/get/len/blob_len/validate_integrity/from_raw_bytes as appropriate. Required proof: cargo test -p redis-ds intset and cargo check --workspace.

allowed_collateral.why: Update generated draft status if consumed.

note: Integrate a read-only legacy Ziplist decoder/iterator/validator. Remove the generated draft's UTF-8 conversion; Redis payloads stay bytes. Mutating ziplist operations may remain explicit TODO(port-wire) if tests and public surface are read-only. Required proof: cargo test -p redis-ds ziplist and cargo check --workspace.

allowed_collateral.why: Update generated draft status if consumed.

note: Integrate useful adlist behavior into canonical LinkedList. Prefer a simple safe Rust shape over the draft's C-pointer mimicry if it preserves append/prepend/delete/index/search/iteration semantics. Required proof: cargo test -p redis-ds adlist and cargo check --workspace.
Run the redis-ds unit suite after the first source-shaped encoding wave.

allowed_collateral.why: Only to call newly canonical ListPack helpers; do not redesign ListPack here.
Update generated draft status if consumed.

note: Land a bounded QuickList MVP after ListPack exists. Use generated draft as API checklist, but implement only push/pop/index/iteration/count and simple node storage. No LZF compression, bookmarks, or object-model wiring. Required proof: cargo test -p redis-ds quicklist and cargo check --workspace.

allowed_collateral.why: Update generated draft status if consumed.

note: Use upstream rax and generated draft to land a safe behavior-first RadixTree API if feasible: insert/find/remove/len/prefix or lexicographic iteration over byte keys. Do not literal-port packed node pointer arithmetic. If full behavior is too large, leave a precise architecture note and tests for the currently implemented subset rather than many todo! bodies. Required proof: cargo test -p redis-ds rax and cargo check --workspace.
Run the redis-ds unit suite after QuickList/Rax source-shaped integration.
